title: claytonia palustris
intro: Claytonia palustris, also known as the marsh purslane or spring purslane, is a perennial plant that belongs to the Portulacaceae family. It is native to North America and is often found growing in moist areas, such as marshes and along streams. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial compounds. Claytonia palustris can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or salves, and is believed to offer health benefits such as soothing digestive issues and reducing inflammation, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
